channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [CNCRD] Concord Control FY26: Turnover ₹210.47 Cr, Profit ₹42.40 Cr
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Concord Control Systems Ltd announced its audited financial results for the year ended March 31, 2026. The company reported a turnover of ₹210.47 crores and a profit of ₹42.40 crores for FY26. The board of directors approved the standalone and consolidated financial results in a meeting held on May 13, 2026. The audit report, issued by M/s Seth & Associates, Chartered Accountants, contained an unmodified opinion.

  • Concord Control Systems' FY26 turnover reached ₹210.47 crores.
  • The company's profit for FY26 increased to ₹42.40 crores.
  • Audited standalone and consolidated financial results for FY26 approved.
  • M/s Seth & Associates issued an unmodified audit opinion.
  • Board meeting was held on May 13, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Concord Control Systems Ltd | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [DHATRE] Dhatre Udyog Fined ₹6.56 Lakhs by BSE for Disclosure Lapses
item.description:
Dhatre Udyog Ltd has been fined ₹6,56,080 by BSE Limited for non-submission of required disclosures under Regulations 33 (March 2024 quarter) and 17(1) and 17(1A) (June 2020 and September 2020 quarters) of the SEBI Listing Regulations, 2015. The company is seeking waiver/reduction of the fine, particularly for non-compliances before the implementation of the Resolution Plan. Dhatre Udyog asserts that the fine does not have a material impact on its financials or operations.

  • BSE imposed a fine of ₹6,56,080 on Dhatre Udyog for disclosure non-compliance.
  • The fine relates to Regulation 33 and 17(1)/17(1A) violations across multiple quarters.
  • Dhatre Udyog is seeking a waiver/reduction of the imposed fine.
  • The company claims no material impact on financials or operations due to the fine.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Dhatre Udyog Ltd | Category: Tax & Penalty

item.title: [JIYAECO] Jiya Eco-Products: Company Profile Submission
item.description:
Jiya Eco-Products Ltd submitted a detailed company profile in line with the erstwhile Information Memorandum, as per Regulation 35 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015. The company underwent Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) initiated on 24.04.2023, with Mr. Pradeep Khandagale's resolution plan approved on 11.12.2024. The filing includes details of erstwhile and current board of directors, as well as promoter shareholding.

  • Jiya Eco-Products submits company profile as per SEBI regulations.
  • CIRP was initiated on 24.04.2023 and concluded with plan approval on 11.12.2024.
  • Pradeep Khandagale's resolution plan was approved by NCLT.
  • Details of pre and post-CIRP board of directors are provided.
  • Promoter shareholding details are included in the filing.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Jiya Eco-Products Ltd | Category: Business Update

item.title: [UCIL] Unifinz Capital to Issue ₹45 Cr NCDs via Private Placement
item.description:
Unifinz Capital India Ltd's Finance Committee approved the issuance of 45,000 listed, rated, senior, secured, taxable, transferable, redeemable, non-convertible debentures (NCDs) with a face value of ₹10,000 each, totaling ₹45 Crore, including a green shoe option of up to ₹30 Crore. These debentures, denominated in Indian Rupees, will be issued on a private placement basis and are proposed to be listed on the Wholesale Debt Market segment of BSE Limited. The NCDs have a tenure of 24 months, with a maturity date of May 20, 2028, and offer a coupon rate of 13% per annum payable monthly.

  • Unifinz Capital approves issuance of ₹45 Cr NCDs via private placement.
  • NCDs have a face value of ₹10,000 each and a 13% coupon rate.
  • The debentures will be listed on the BSE Wholesale Debt Market.
  • NCD tenure is 24 months, maturing on May 20, 2028.
  • The issue includes a green shoe option of up to ₹30 Crore.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Unifinz Capital India Ltd | Category: Loan & Debt

item.title: [7SEASL] 7Seas Entertainment: Monitoring Agency Report for Mar 2026
item.description:
7Seas Entertainment Ltd has submitted a Monitoring Agency Report by Brickwork Ratings for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, regarding the utilization of funds raised through a preferential issue. The funds raised amounted to ₹17.32 Crore through equity shares and convertible warrants. The report indicates no deviation from the stated objectives for the use of these funds, as per SEBI (ICDR) Regulations, 2018.

  • 7Seas Entertainment submits Monitoring Agency Report for quarter ended March 31, 2026.
  • Report by Brickwork Ratings covers utilization of funds from preferential issue.
  • Funds raised through preferential issue totaled ₹17.32 Crore.
  • No deviation reported from the intended use of funds.
  • Report complies with SEBI (ICDR) Regulations, 2018.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: 7Seas Entertainment Ltd | Category: Regulatory

item.title: [NCCBLUE] NCC Bluewater Board Meeting on May 21, 2026
item.description:
NCC Bluewater Products Ltd has announced a meeting of its Board of Directors scheduled for May 21, 2026, to consider and approve the audited financial results/statements for the fourth quarter and financial year ended March 31, 2026. The meeting will be held at the company's registered office.

  • Board meeting scheduled for May 21, 2026.
  • Meeting to approve audited financial results for Q4 and FY26.
  • Meeting will be held at the company's registered office.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: NCC BLUE WATER PRODUCTS LTD. | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [EUREKAFORBE] Eureka Forbes Board Meeting on May 19, 2026
item.description:
Eureka Forbes Ltd has scheduled a Board of Directors meeting on May 19, 2026, to consider and approve the Audited Standalone and Consolidated Financial Statements and Results of the Company for the Quarter and Year ended March 31, 2026. The trading window remains closed until 48 hours after the results are publicly available, as per insider trading regulations.

  • Eureka Forbes board to approve audited financial results.
  • Meeting scheduled for May 19, 2026.
  • Trading window closed until 48 hours post result announcement.
  • Consolidated and Standalone results to be considered.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Eureka Forbes Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [VGUARD] V-Guard Faces ₹68.24 Lakhs Duty & Penalty
item.description:
V-Guard Industries Ltd received an order from the Commissioner of Customs (NS-V), JNCH, regarding misclassification of certain product parts. The order re-classifies the goods, confirming a differential duty demand of ₹68,24,372 with applicable interest under Section 28AA. Additionally, a penalty of ₹68,24,372 has been imposed under Section 114A, with a conditional option to reduce it to 25%. The imports covered in the order relate to Bills of Entry spanning from May 2020 to July 2024. V-Guard is evaluating the order and plans to file an appeal before CESTAT within the prescribed time limits.

  • V-Guard receives order for misclassification of product parts.
  • Differential duty of ₹68.24 lakhs confirmed with interest.
  • Penalty of ₹68.24 lakhs imposed, potentially reducible to 25%.
  • Order relates to imports from May 2020 to July 2024.
  • V-Guard to appeal the order before CESTAT.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: V-GUARD INDUSTRIES LTD. | Category: Tax & Penalty

item.title: [BAJAJ-AUTO] Bajaj Mobility AG Q1 2026: Revenue Up 70.2% to EUR 331.3 Mn
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Bajaj Auto Ltd's step-down subsidiary, Bajaj Mobility AG (formerly PIERER Mobility AG), reported a significant improvement in operating performance for Q1 2026. Consolidated revenue increased by 70.2% to EUR 331.3 million. Motorcycle sales rose by 125.1% to 40,332 units, with motorcycle revenue up by 151.6%. EBITDA turned positive at EUR 5.5 million, and EBIT improved by EUR 65.5 million. The company successfully completed a EUR 550 million refinancing of KTM AG. Additional restructuring measures, including a workforce reduction of 500 employees, are expected to be completed by Q3 2026.

  • Q1 2026 revenue increased by 70.2% to EUR 331.3 million.
  • Motorcycle sales increased by 125.1% to 40,332 units.
  • EBITDA turned positive at EUR 5.5 million.
  • KTM AG completed a EUR 550 million refinancing.
  • Workforce reduction of 500 employees planned by Q3 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BAJAJ AUTO LTD. | Category: Quarterly Updates

item.title: [SHILCTECH] Shilchar Tech: Tax Appeal Sets Aside ₹3.17 Cr Demand
item.description:
Shilchar Technologies Ltd. received an order from the Commissioner of Central Tax (Appeals), CGST and Central Excise, Vadodara, regarding a demand of ₹3,19,24,454. The order, dated April 30, 2026, and received on May 12, 2026, set aside a demand of ₹3,17,73,217, including directions to re-determine a demand of ₹1,64,50,315 under Section 73 of the CGST Act, 2017. The interest and penalty related to the set-aside tax demand were also waived. The company states that the order does not have a material impact on its financial position or operations.

  • Commissioner of Central Tax (Appeals) set aside a demand of ₹3.17 Cr for Shilchar Technologies.
  • The order includes re-determination of a demand of ₹1.64 Cr under Section 73 of the CGST Act.
  • Interest and penalty related to the set-aside tax demand have also been waived.
  • Shilchar Technologies states the order does not have a material financial impact.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SHILCHAR TECHNOLOGIES LTD. | Category: Tax & Penalty
